result.response_content_preview:
ACTION:
FinalizeIdea

ARGUMENTS:
{"idea": {"Name": "spectral_precision_routing", "Title": "Spectral Precision Routing: Training-Free Dynamic Bit-Width Allocation via Activation Geometry Diagnostics", "Short Hypothesis": The spectral condition number and Fisher discriminant ratio of layer-wise activation covariances directly quantify a model's sensitivity to quantization noise, enabling a training-free routing framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision (FP16, BF16, INT8) at inference time. This direction is necessary because existing mixed-precision methods rely on expensive training-time calibration or opaque learned controllers that cannot adapt to real-time distribution shifts or enforce strict latency budgets. Activation geometry provides the optimal setting to investigate this, as subspace stability metrics inherently bound decision boundary robustness under numerical perturbation, allowing rigorous PAC-Bayes verification and system-aware latency optimization without
